rationale: I split Public Health & Epidemiology into 14 practitioner-facing subspecialties to balance breadth (policy, environment, global) with methodologic depth (epidemiology, biostatistics, surveillance) while keeping cards focused and discoverable. I kept large, commonly‑used professional labels (e.g., Health Policy, Biostatistics, Global Health) rather than idiosyncratic academic names so users immediately recognize their field. I deliberately separated methodological areas (Epidemiology, Biostatistics, Surveillance) from application areas (Infectious, Chronic, Maternal & Child) to make mapping papers unambiguous. Some borderline splits — e.g., Health Services Research vs Implementation Science — were retained as distinct because practitioners introduce themselves that way and because their paper audiences and methods differ.

subspecialties.name: Infectious Disease Epidemiology
subspecialties.tagline: Transmission, outbreaks, vaccines, pathogen surveillance and modeling
covers: Epidemiology of infections, outbreak investigations, vaccine effectiveness studies
Examples: COVID/SARS/MERS studies, influenza surveillance, antimicrobial resistance spread
explicitly_excludes: surveillance-and-informatics — routine public health surveillance infrastructure (see that category)
biology — laboratory pathogen discovery and molecular virology

subspecialties.name: Chronic Disease Epidemiology
subspecialties.tagline: Population studies of noncommunicable diseases, risk factors and prevention
covers: Epidemiology of cardiovascular disease, diabetes, cancer, respiratory disease
Examples: cohort studies, risk-factor associations, screening and prevention trials
explicitly_excludes: maternal-and-child-health — papers focused primarily on MCH outcomes
health-services-research — system-level delivery/quality studies

subspecialties.name: Epidemiology
subspecialties.tagline: Study design, causal inference, exposure assessment, surveillance methods
covers: Methods papers on study design, bias, causal inference, exposure measurement
Examples: case‑control/crossover design papers, DAGs, measurement error methods
explicitly_excludes: biostatistics — mathematical/statistical methodology and algorithm development
implementation-science — adaptation/scale-up of interventions

subspecialties.name: Biostatistics
subspecialties.tagline: Statistical methods, models, inference, and applied data analysis for health
covers: Development or application of statistical models, Bayesian methods, machine learning for public health
Examples: survival models, missing data techniques, causal machine‑learning methods
explicitly_excludes: epidemiology — study design and causal questions framed by epidemiologists
natural-language-processing / informatics work focusing on tools rather than statistics

subspecialties.name: Health Policy
subspecialties.tagline: Policy analysis, regulation, governance, health systems financing and management
covers: Policy evaluation, comparative health systems, legislation effects, governance research
Examples: analyses of insurance policy changes, national policy impact studies, regulatory evaluations
explicitly_excludes: health-economics — microeconomic evaluation and cost-effectiveness modeling
health-services-research — operational delivery and quality improvement unless policy‑focused

subspecialties.name: Global Health
subspecialties.tagline: Health in low- and middle-income settings, global programs, equity and diplomacy
covers: Program evaluations, global burden studies, cross-country health system comparisons
Examples: implementation of malaria campaigns, global maternal health interventions, WHO/global surveillance analyses
explicitly_excludes: environmental-health — environment-driven exposures studied at local or global levels
health-policy — national/regional policy work not framed as global health

subspecialties.name: Environmental Health
subspecialties.tagline: Environmental exposures, pollution, climate impacts, and exposure assessment
covers: Air/water/soil pollution, chemical exposures, climate change impacts on health
Examples: air pollution cohort studies, heatwave mortality, lead exposure assessments
explicitly_excludes: occupational-health — workplace exposures and occupational disease
biology — mechanistic toxicology studies in lab settings

subspecialties.name: Occupational Health
subspecialties.tagline: Workplace exposures, occupational safety, and worker health surveillance
covers: Occupational exposure assessments, workplace injury prevention, occupational epidemiology
Examples: silica/asbestos exposure studies, shift work and health outcomes, workplace intervention trials
explicitly_excludes: environmental-health — community environmental exposures outside workplace contexts
medicine — clinical occupational medicine case reports

subspecialties.name: Health Economics
subspecialties.tagline: Cost-effectiveness, economic evaluation, and resource allocation in health
covers: Cost-effectiveness analyses, economic modeling, budget impact and resource allocation studies
Examples: QALY/CUA studies, valuation of interventions, health financing analyses
explicitly_excludes: health-policy — policy process and governance studies without microeconomic evaluation
biostatistics — purely statistical methodological papers

subspecialties.name: Implementation Science
subspecialties.tagline: Scaling, adaptation, fidelity and real-world uptake of interventions
covers: Studies of implementation strategies, process evaluation, pragmatic trials and hybrid designs
Examples: scale-up of vaccination programs, fidelity assessments, implementation frameworks
explicitly_excludes: health-services-research — organizational delivery/performance studies unless focused on implementation methods
global-health — if primary framing is country/global programs rather than implementation science methods

subspecialties.name: Health Services Research
subspecialties.tagline: Delivery, quality, access, equity, and organization of health care services
covers: Studies of care delivery, quality improvement, access disparities, hospital/system performance
Examples: care pathway evaluations, readmission reduction studies, workforce analyses
explicitly_excludes: health-policy — system-level policy analysis if framed as policy rather than delivery
implementation-science — research primarily about implementation strategies and methods

subspecialties.name: Maternal & Child Health
subspecialties.tagline: Population research on pregnancy, newborns, children, and adolescent health
covers: Perinatal epidemiology, pediatric population studies, nutrition and early-life interventions
Examples: antenatal care program evaluations, childhood vaccination coverage research
explicitly_excludes: neonatology/pediatrics (medicine) — clinical patient‑level care studies
global-health — when the primary frame is programmatic/global policy

subspecialties.name: Injury & Violence Prevention
subspecialties.tagline: Unintentional injury, violence, injury epidemiology and prevention programs
covers: Road traffic injuries, domestic violence epidemiology, injury surveillance and prevention trials
Examples: seatbelt policy evaluations, community violence intervention studies
explicitly_excludes: occupational-health — workplace injuries studied within occupational settings
medicine — clinical trauma care research

subspecialties.name: Public Health Surveillance & Informatics
subspecialties.tagline: Disease surveillance systems, digital epidemiology, data platforms and ML for public health
covers: Design and evaluation of surveillance systems, public health informatics, digital data streams and analytic platforms
Examples: syndromic surveillance, EHR-based surveillance, wastewater surveillance, ML for outbreak detection
explicitly_excludes: biostatistics — statistical method development not tied to surveillance systems
infectious-disease-epidemiology — outbreak science focused on pathogen dynamics rather than systems/tools

uncovered_concerns: Mental health epidemiology: considered as its own card but many papers sit within chronic disease or health services; kept under Chronic Disease or HSR to avoid fragmentation.
Digital health / mHealth: overlaps strongly with Surveillance & Informatics and Implementation Science; not split out to reduce card proliferation.
Equity & social determinants: intentionally not a separate subspecialty because it is a cross-cutting lens applied across multiple categories (policy, HSR, global, chronic).
Clinical/individual-patient care studies: explicitly routed to Medicine level‑1 rather than kept here to preserve MECE.
